stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results (Apr 21): Net profit Rs 655.80 cr (+19.15% YoY); GNPA fell below 1% to 0.93% (improved underwriting); retail loan assets Rs 86,946 cr (+16% YoY); Q4 retail disbursement Rs 9,020 cr (+32% YoY); Rs 8/share FY26 dividend declared. Stock surged ~9% on results day, opening at Rs 949.50 and touching Rs 1,007 intraday. Market cap Rs 27,279 cr (Apr 27). Trading near Rs 990-1,045 range late April/early May. ICICI Securities upgraded to BUY with target Rs 1,350 (from Rs 990); MOFSL also Buy.
stocks.sector_context: HFC sector benefits from RBI's cumulative 125 bps rate cuts through FY25 with continued easing bias - supports loan affordability and disbursement growth. Bank lending to NBFCs accelerated to +19.1% YoY in Feb 2026 (from 6.4% prior). Indian home loan market USD 430.7 bn (2026) projected to USD 809 bn by 2031 (~13.4% CAGR). NBFC growth trajectory ~18.4% through 2031. Headwind: HFC cost-of-funds may rise marginally on weak deposit transmission; early-2026 housing demand softened ~7% YoY.
stocks.peer_comparison: Lags Can Fin Homes on margins/asset quality - PNBHF Q4 NIM ~3.69% (compressed YoY) vs Can Fin 4.19% (well above HFC sector avg 3.0-3.5%). Can Fin FY26 PAT Rs 1,086 cr (+27% YoY) with ROE 23.12%, GNPA 0.85% - cleaner book. PNBHF P/E ~10.7x richer than LIC Housing Finance ~5.3x but earnings growth (+19%) ahead of LIC HF. Opex up 17% YoY suggests cost discipline weaker than peers.
red_flags: Q4 PAT beat aided by provision write-backs - earnings quality lower than headline suggests
NIM compression vs YoY; cost-of-funds risk if deposit competition rises
Opex grew 17% YoY - faster than NII growth (+10.8%)
Stock up ~9-11% post-results - near-term mean-reversion risk
Lags Can Fin Homes peer on NIM, ROE, asset quality
catalysts: ICICI Sec target Rs 1,350 implies ~30% upside vs CMP
Affordable housing book +61% YoY - high-growth segment scale-up
Mgmt guidance >20% loan book growth FY27
RBI rate-cut cycle supports affordability/disbursements
Dual brokerage upgrades (ICICI Sec + MOFSL Buy)

stocks.recent_news: Nippon India ETF Gold BeES trading Rs 122-123 May 2-4, 2026 (NAV Rs 123.43 May 2); 52-week range Rs 76.36-148.14. Tracks domestic gold price. Gold trading $4,613/oz May 3 (May range expected $4,380-5,100). India 24K gold ~Rs 15,093/gram. Indian gold ETF AUM hit Rs 1.71 lakh cr March 2026 (+191% YoY); FY26 net inflows Rs 68,868 cr - more than the prior 5 years combined. GOLDBEES is largest Indian gold ETF (~Rs 1.27 lakh cr AUM). 1-year return ~30%; Q1 2026 MCX gold +20% q/q, +81% y/y to record Rs 1.51 lakh/10g.
stocks.sector_context: Gold benefiting from geopolitical tensions (US-Iran-Israel), persistent central bank reserve buying (~585 tonnes/quarter avg in 2026), and Fed rate uncertainty. Only 5.1% probability of June Fed cut; 94.9% expect rates unchanged at 3.50-3.75%. JP Morgan/SSGA targeting $5,000/oz by Q4 2026; some forecasts $5,400-6,000/oz year-end. India launched additional fund March 2026 (now 26 gold ETFs). Gold ETF inflows briefly eclipsed equity fund inflows. WGC: Indian bar/coin demand and ETFs strong on price momentum + risk hedging.
stocks.peer_comparison: Largest of 26 Indian gold ETFs by AUM (Rs 1.27 lakh cr); peers include HDFC Gold ETF, SBI Gold ETF. Lowest tracking error among major peers historically. Expense ratio 0.80% - moderate. Liquidity highest among gold ETFs. No significant return divergence vs peers - all track gold price net of fees.
red_flags: Stretched price action - 1Y return ~30%; risk of pullback if Fed surprises hawkish
Gold positioning crowded - record ETF inflows could reverse if INR weakens vs USD reverses
Tracking error risk if INR/USD moves out of sync with international gold
catalysts: Fed pivot/rate-cut surprise could trigger gold breakout to $5,000+
Continued central bank buying floor (~585 tonnes/qtr)
Geopolitical risk premium (Middle East tensions)
Festive/wedding season demand for physical gold supports ETF AUM
JP Morgan/SSGA Q4 2026 $5,000 target

stocks.recent_news: Stock hit all-time high Rs 1,724.10 May 4 (intraday Rs 1,748.80); +25% in past month; +6% on May 4. Q4 FY26 (Apr 30): Consolidated PAT Rs 3,308 cr (+9% YoY) on revenue Rs 10,738 cr (+26% YoY). FY26: Revenue Rs 38,736 cr (+25%); EBITDA Rs 22,851 cr (+20%); cargo volume crossed 500 MMT (500.8 MMT, +11% YoY) - first Indian operator. Logistics +55%, marine +134%. April 2026 cargo +15% YoY to 43.1 MMT (containers/dry cargo +17%). Board declared Rs 7.50 dividend (record date June 12); AGM June 24, 2026. Brokerage targets: MOFSL Buy Rs 1,900; Elara Accumulate Rs 1,883; Axis Direct Rs 1,790-1,850.
stocks.sector_context: Indian port/logistics sector benefiting from rising export volumes, infrastructure capex push, and PLI-led manufacturing growth. APSEZ holds 45.5% container market share. Logistics rail volume softer (-16% YoY in April for APSEZ TEUs) - segment-specific weakness. Domestic ports contributed Rs 25,755 cr to FY26 revenue (+13%). Adani Group basket strength on May 4 with Power and Green also surging.
stocks.peer_comparison: APSEZ retains dominance vs JSW Infra and Gujarat Pipavav - 500+ MMT cargo (industry-leading); 45.5% container share. Strong premium valuation justified by integrated transport model (ports + logistics + marine). FY26 EBITDA growth +20% well above sector average. Outperformed Nifty +25% in past month vs broader index modest gains.
red_flags: Stock +25% in a month - extended technically; near-term mean-reversion risk
Logistics rail TEU volume -16% YoY in April - segment weakness
Elara downgraded from Buy to Accumulate - some brokers cooling on upside
Adani group concentration / governance overhang persists despite SEBI clean chit (Sep 2025)
Q4 PAT growth +9% materially below revenue growth +26% - margin pressure
catalysts: All-time high momentum + 5%+ daily gains attracting flow
AGM June 24 - dividend Rs 7.50 record date June 12
FY26 outperformed guidance - sets up for strong FY27 outlook
MOFSL Buy target Rs 1,900 (~10% upside)
First Indian operator to cross 500 MMT - sentiment milestone

stocks.recent_news: April 2026 record monthly sales 239,646 units (+33.29% YoY); domestic 191,122 (highest-ever); compact segment 80,659; exports 40,054. Stock +5% to intraday Rs 13,976 on May 4 on April sales beat. Q4 FY26 (released late April): Consolidated PAT Rs 3,659 cr (-6.5% YoY) DESPITE revenue Rs 52,462 cr (+28.2% YoY) - margin compression. Gross margin -312 bps YoY to 13.12%; PAT margin -276 bps to 7.31%. Higher RM costs (steel, precious metals for catalytic converters), lower other income. Rs 140/share dividend declared.
stocks.sector_context: Indian PV market FY27 strong start - April 2026 ~4.5 lakh units (+12.94% YoY) vs 3.54 lakh prior year. Tata Motors +30.5% YoY (59,000 units, #2); Mahindra +8% YoY (56,331); Bajaj Auto +40% total. Tata EV +72-77% YoY shows EV momentum (where Maruti is laggard). Sector benefiting from RBI rate cuts + monsoon optimism + festive carryover. Raw material cost inflation pressuring margins industry-wide.
stocks.peer_comparison: Maruti 35% YoY growth led the industry vs Tata Motors +30.5% and Mahindra +8% in April. Domestic PV market share intact at #1. However, Maruti lags on EV: Tata EV +77% YoY in April, Maruti EV ~1,200 units. Margin compression sharper than premium peers given heavy small-car/compact mix exposed to raw material costs. Tata's mix-shift to SUVs/EVs delivers better margins than Maruti's compact-heavy book.
red_flags: Q4 PAT -6.5% YoY despite +28% revenue growth - severe margin compression
Gross margin -312 bps YoY; raw material costs (steel/precious metals) elevated
EV laggard - lost share to Tata in fastest-growing segment
Compact segment exposure highest among peers - lowest margins, most RM-sensitive
Stock has run up post April sales - much of volume catalyst priced in
catalysts: Record April sales 239,646 units (+33% YoY) - momentum carries into May
Highest-ever monthly domestic 191,122 - market share defense intact
Rs 140/share dividend supports income holders
Auto sector tailwind from RBI rate cuts + monsoon optimism
Compact segment (Baleno/Swift/Dzire) demand resilience

stocks.recent_news: Stock hit 52-week high Rs 199.59 (Apr 29) post-results; +10% rally; trading Rs 206.90 May 4. Q4 FY26: Net profit Rs 534.14 cr (+68% YoY from Rs 317.90 cr). NIM 6.2% (-46 bps YoY but +30 bps QoQ; cost of funds fell 39 bps). GNPA fell 140 bps YoY to 3.27%; NNPA improved to 0.97% (-32 bps YoY). Collection efficiency 98.9% (vs 98.1% prior). Total deposits Rs 1.66 lakh cr (+10% YoY). Credit costs at 2%, guidance to 1.6-1.7%. Mgmt guides 15-17% advances CAGR. Brokerage targets: Emkay Buy Rs 220 (raised 22%); Nuvama Hold Rs 190 (raised from Rs 133); UBS Neutral Rs 200; CLSA upgrade with raised target.
stocks.sector_context: Indian private banks Q4 FY26: HDFC Bank PAT +9.1%, ICICI +8.5% (NIM 4.32%, GNPA record-low 1.40%), Axis credit growth 12-16%, IndusInd PAT Rs 594 cr but credit -8.7% YoY. Asset quality improved across major banks. Microfinance industry still under stress: sector PAR 180+ rose to 14.9% (Jun 2025) from 12.6% (Mar 2025). MFI election cycle risk from Bengal + Assam state elections - historically loan-waiver discourse weakens repayments.
stocks.peer_comparison: Bandhan NIM 6.2% well above private bank average (~3.5-4.5%) reflecting MFI mix - but offset by higher credit costs/asset risk. PAT growth +68% YoY outpaces all major private banks (HDFC +9%, ICICI +8.5%). GNPA 3.27% materially worse than ICICI 1.40% / HDFC quality - reflects MFI exposure. ROA improvement trajectory but absolute level still trails best-in-class peers. Trading at a discount to peers on P/B reflecting MFI/election concentration.
red_flags: MFI book remains majority exposure with sector-wide PAR 180+ at 14.9%
West Bengal/Assam election cycle - historical loan-waiver narrative risk
El Nino monsoon risk could pressure rural collections
NIM 6.2% still -46 bps YoY despite QoQ improvement
GNPA 3.27% materially elevated vs private bank peers (1.4-2%)
Earlier reports of promoter group exploring stake sale / holdco IPO
catalysts: Multiple brokerage upgrades post Q4 (Emkay Rs 220, CLSA upgrade)
PAT +68% YoY - strongest among private banks
Asset quality inflection: GNPA -140 bps YoY, collections 98.9%
Mgmt guides 30-40 bps NIM expansion next 3 quarters
Credit cost glide-path to 1.6-1.7% (from 2%) - operating leverage
15-17% advances CAGR guidance
